3 | P a g e
Healthy Eating Plan
Phase 1 – Just Start Counting! – Harris Benedict Equation
Calorie Tracking Method: 	For Women
Step 1: Enter Stats 	655 + ( 4.35 x ______lbs ) + ( 4.7 x _______inches ) - ( 4.7 x ______years old )
Step 2: Do the Math 	655 + 	__________ 	+ 	_____________ 	- 	__________ 	= 	BMR
Step 3: BMR x 1.2 	BMR _______ 	x 	1.2 = 	_________ Calories need to maintain current weight
Step 4: Change calories based on goals ____________ Calories – 	500* =
Calorie Tracking Method: 	For Men
Step 1: Enter Stats 	66 + ( 6.23 x ______lbs ) + ( 12.7 x _______inches ) - ( 6.8 x ______years old )
Step 2: Do the Math 	66 + 	__________ 	+ 	_____________ 	- 	__________ 	= 	BMR
Step 3: BMR x 1.2 	BMR _______ 	x 	1.2 = 	_________ Calories need to maintain current weight
Step 4: Change calories based on goals ____________ Calories – 	500* =
*1 pound of fat weighs 3500 calories. 3500 divided by 7 (days a week) = 500 calories per day or 1 pound per week.
Use the MyFitnessPal App or Website to count Calories. Or manually count through food labels.
Phase 2 – Break it down by meal to boost metabolism!
Calorie Breakdown by Meal
Breakfast 	Snack #1 	Lunch 	Snack #2 	Dinner
Calorie Goal: 	Calorie Goal: 	Calorie Goal: 	Calorie Goal: 	Calorie Goal:
Phase 3 – Break it down by source to get lean and fuel your workouts!
Calorie Breakdown by Source
Carbohydrates
Fat
Protein
40%
20%
_________Calorie Goal Per Day
_________Calorie Goal Per Day
40%
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

4 | P a g e
Workout Plan
Our goal is to burn 3500 calories each week by working out and training hard! As we learned from our
diet plan, that could mean at least another pound of fat lost, or a pound of muscle gained!
Calories burned per workout routine:
• 	Light Cardio per 30 minutes (walking, recumbent bike, ect.) 	115 Calories
• 	Moderate Cardio per 30 minutes (jogging, elliptical, stationary bike) 	300 Calories
• 	Group Exercise Classes per 30 minutes (Zumba, PowerFlex, BodyPump) 	325 Calories
• 	High Intensity Group Classes per 30 minutes (Kick-Boxing, TRX, Spin, ect.) 	400 Calories
• 	Our Proprietary At-Home and in Gym Workouts! 	400 Calories
• 	Our HTP Semi-Private Training 	800 Calories
The reason why our training burns so many calories is that it’s the only tailored to you workout routine
on the list. Other places exercises focus on one aspect of fitness, i.e. cardiovascular, lower body, upper
body, core, ect. When you use the entire body and all of the muscles your able to burn WAY more
calories and get much stronger, much faster! Resistance training burns fat quickly.
How are we going to burn 3500 calories each week?
Monday 	Tuesday 	Wednesday 	Thursday 	Friday 	Saturday 	Sunday
Workout: 	Workout: 	Workout: 	Workout: 	Workout: 	Workout: 	Workout:
Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned: 	Calories Burned:
We’re going to send you the link for our proprietary At-Home workouts!
After each workout you complete mark it on your Weekly Tracker by writing on a scale of 1 to
10 how exhausted you are after finishing.
Total Calories burned per week: 	Specific area’s you would like to focus on: 	What’s your ideal off day?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

5 | P a g e
Recovery Plan
Sleep: 8 hours is recommended to allow your body to rebuild and recover. How many are you currently sleeping?
Water : We recommend you drink 3 liters or 100 ounces of water each day. Drinking 2 cups before each meal will
get you half way there and encourage you to eat less!
Dietary Supplements: Supplements can 10x your results. We recommend:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 6 --

6 | P a g e
Accountability Plan
We’ll be reaching out to throughout the week to make sure everything is going according to
plan. In addition to that, we’d like you to fill out this Weekly Tracker and send it to us each
week when it’s complete! Here’s an example:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
